entity.name: Browser support
entity.description: Browser support, compatibility, and consistency issues.
entity.descriptionHtml: Browser support, compatibility, and consistency issues.
entity.descriptionClean: Browser support, compatibility, and consistency issues.

webFeature.description_html: The light-dark() CSS function accepts two colors and uses one depending on the current color scheme.
webFeature.name: light-dark()

example.codeHighlighted: .foo { background-color: lab(29.2345% 39.3825 20.0664); background-color: lch(29.2345% 44.2 27); }

webFeature.description_html: The Oklab color space expresses colors in terms of lightness and how red/green and blue/yellow a color is, aiming to match how humans perceive colors. OkLCh is a variant of Oklab with polar coordinates. These color spaces can be used with the CSS oklab() and oklch() functions.
webFeature.name: Oklab and OkLCh

example.codeHighlighted: .foo { --color: #4488dd; background-color: hsl(from var(--color) h s calc(l * 1.2)) }

resources.title: Create a color theme with CSS Relative Color Syntax, CSS color-mix(), and CSS color-contrast()

webFeature.description_html: The from keyword for color functions (color(), hsl(), oklch(), etc.) creates a new color based on a given color by modifying the values of the input color. Also known as relative color syntax (RCS).
webFeature.name: Relative colors

example.codeHighlighted: .teal { background: color-mix(in lab, white 20%, #34c9eb); }

webFeature.description_html: The color-mix() function mixes two colors in a given color space and by a given amount. Commonly, lighter or darker variations of a color are created by mixing with white or black.
webFeature.name: color-mix()

webFeature.description_html: The contrast-color() CSS function picks a color that has guaranteed contrast against a specified foreground or background color.
webFeature.name: contrast-color()

webFeature.description_html: Custom properties are CSS properties prefixed with -- that set values you can reuse with the var() function. For example, you can set a --key-color property to reuse as border-color: var(--key-color). Also known as CSS variables.
webFeature.name: Custom properties

entity.name: Choice overload
entity.description: Overwhelming amount of options and choices
entity.descriptionHtml: Overwhelming amount of options and choices
entity.descriptionClean: Overwhelming amount of options and choices

webFeature.description_html: The color-scheme CSS property sets which color schemes (light or dark) an element uses and may prevent automatic dark mode adjustments by the browser.
webFeature.name: color-scheme

webFeature.description_html: The currentColor CSS value allows you to reuse an element's computed text color for other properties.
webFeature.name: currentColor

webFeature.description_html: The accent-color CSS property sets a color for checkboxes, radio buttons, and other form controls.
webFeature.name: accent-color

example.codeHighlighted: a:has(> img) { border: 2px solid grey; }

webFeature.description_html: The :has() CSS functional pseudo-class matches an element if any of the selectors passed as parameters would match at least one element.
webFeature.name: :has()

entity.description: Obstacles in providing accessible experiences (regardless of cause), assistive‑tech compatibility, etc.

entity.name: Poor learnability
entity.description: High learning curve, requires too much knowledge to get started, or too much information to memorize.

entity.name: Excessive verbosity
entity.description: APIs being overly verbose and/or requiring too much boilerplate.
entity.descriptionHtml: APIs being overly verbose and/or requiring too much boilerplate.
entity.descriptionClean: APIs being overly verbose and/or requiring too much boilerplate.

entity.description: Not clear why you would use a specific feature

entity.descriptionClean: Not clear why you would use a specific feature

entity.description: Features, libraries, and APIs changing too fast
entity.descriptionHtml: Features, libraries, and APIs changing too fast
entity.descriptionClean: Features, libraries, and APIs changing too fast

entity.name: Browser vendor issues
entity.description: Browser support issues, implementation bugs, cross‑browser implementation differences or inconsistencies, browser prioritization, speed of adoption of new features, any issues where specific browsers, browser engines, or browser companies are mentioned (and it’s not to praise them).

resources.title: Create a color theme with CSS Relative Color Syntax, CSS color-mix(), and CSS color-contrast()

webFeature.description_html: The from keyword for color functions (color(), hsl(), oklch(), etc.) creates a new color based on a given color by modifying the values of the input color. Also known as relative color syntax (RCS).
webFeature.name: Relative colors

webFeature.description_html: Custom properties are CSS properties prefixed with -- that set values you can reuse with the var() function. For example, you can set a --key-color property to reuse as border-color: var(--key-color). Also known as CSS variables.
webFeature.name: Custom properties

entity.name: Tooling
entity.description: Issues around third party tools, or lack thereof, including tooling in the browser (extensions, devtools, etc.).
entity.descriptionHtml: Issues around third party tools, or lack thereof, including tooling in the browser (extensions, devtools, etc.).
entity.descriptionClean: Issues around third party tools, or lack thereof, including tooling in the browser (extensions, devtools, etc.).

entity.name: Preprocessor reliance
entity.description: Native CSS still lacking features that Sass/SCSS/PostCSS provide (mixins, @use, selector concatenation), forcing continued reliance on preprocessors.
entity.descriptionHtml: Native CSS still lacking features that Sass/SCSS/PostCSS provide (mixins, @use, selector concatenation), forcing continued reliance on preprocessors.
entity.descriptionClean: Native CSS still lacking features that Sass/SCSS/PostCSS provide (mixins, @use, selector concatenation), forcing continued reliance on preprocessors.

webFeature.description_html: The color-scheme CSS property sets which color schemes (light or dark) an element uses and may prevent automatic dark mode adjustments by the browser.
webFeature.name: color-scheme

webFeature.description_html: The rgb(), rgba(), and hexadecimal (as in #004488ff) notations pick colors using red, green, blue, and alpha (transparency) channels.
webFeature.name: RGB

webFeature.description_html: The linear-gradient() and radial-gradient() CSS functions and their repeating counterparts create backgrounds that progress smoothly between multiple colors.
webFeature.name: Gradients

example.codeHighlighted: .gradient { background: linear-gradient(in oklab, hsl(80 90% 50%), red); }

resources.title: Using Different Color Spaces for Non-Boring Gradients

webFeature.description_html: Gradient interpolation controls how the colors between gradient stops are calculated in CSS. For example, linear-gradient(to right in oklch longer hue, red, red); calculates in the oklch color space, with the hue going all the way around the hue circle from red back to red.
webFeature.name: Gradient interpolation

webFeature.description_html: The color() function picks a color from a given color space. Wide gamut color spaces like display-p3 allow showing more vibrant and saturated colors than the standard srgb color space.
webFeature.name: color()

example.codeHighlighted: .teal { background: color-mix(in lab, white 20%, #34c9eb); }

webFeature.description_html: The color-mix() function mixes two colors in a given color space and by a given amount. Commonly, lighter or darker variations of a color are created by mixing with white or black.
webFeature.name: color-mix()

entity.name: Education
entity.description: Issues related to learning new technologies
entity.descriptionHtml: Issues related to learning new technologies
entity.descriptionClean: Issues related to learning new technologies

example.codeHighlighted: @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { .foo { background: #333; color: white; } }

webFeature.description_html: The prefers-color-scheme CSS media query sets styles based on the requested color scheme, light or dark.
webFeature.name: prefers-color-scheme media query

entity.name: Debugging
